root_text.an5.73:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.73:1.3: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, bhikkhu dhammavihārī hotī”ti?
root_text.an5.73:2.2: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an5.73:2.3: So tāya dhammapariyattiyā divasaṁ atināmeti, riñcati paṭisallānaṁ, nānuyuñjati ajjhattaṁ cetosamathaṁ.
root_text.an5.73:2.4: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhu: ‘bhikkhu pariyattibahulo, no dhammavihārī’.
root_text.an5.73:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhu, bhikkhu yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ vitthārena paresaṁ deseti.
root_text.an5.73:3.2: So tāya dhammapaññattiyā divasaṁ atināmeti, riñcati paṭisallānaṁ, nānuyuñjati ajjhattaṁ cetosamathaṁ.
root_text.an5.73:3.3: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhu: ‘bhikkhu paññattibahulo, no dhammavihārī’.
root_text.an5.73:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhu, bhikkhu yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ vitthārena sajjhāyaṁ karoti.
root_text.an5.73:4.2: So tena sajjhāyena divasaṁ atināmeti, riñcati paṭisallānaṁ, nānuyuñjati ajjhattaṁ cetosamathaṁ.
root_text.an5.73:4.3: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhu: ‘bhikkhu sajjhāyabahulo, no dhammavihārī’.
root_text.an5.73:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhu, bhikkhu yathāsutaṁ yathāpariyattaṁ dhammaṁ cetasā anuvitakketi anuvicāreti manasānupekkhati.
root_text.an5.73:5.2: So tehi dhammavitakkehi divasaṁ atināmeti, riñcati paṭisallānaṁ, nānuyuñjati ajjhattaṁ cetosamathaṁ.
root_text.an5.73:5.3: Ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhu: ‘bhikkhu vitakkabahulo, no dhammavihārī’.
root_text.an5.73:6.2: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an5.73:6.3: So tāya dhammapariyattiyā na divasaṁ atināmeti, nāpi riñcati paṭisallānaṁ, anuyuñjati ajjhattaṁ cetosamathaṁ.
root_text.an5.73:6.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhu, bhikkhu dhammavihārī hoti.
root_text.an5.73:7.1: Iti kho, bhikkhu, desito mayā pariyattibahulo, desito paññattibahulo, desito sajjhāyabahulo, desito vitakkabahulo, desito dhammavihārī.
root_text.an5.73:7.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhu, satthārā karaṇīyaṁ sāvakānaṁ hitesinā anukampakena anukampaṁ upādāya, kataṁ vo taṁ mayā.
root_text.an5.73:7.3: Etāni, bhikkhu, rukkhamūlāni, etāni suññāgārāni. Jhāyatha, bhikkhu, mā pamādattha, mā pacchā vippaṭisārino ahuvattha. Ayaṁ vo amhākaṁ anusāsanī”ti.

translation_text.an5.73:0.3: One Who Lives by the Teaching (1st)
translation_text.an5.73:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an5.73:1.2: “Sir, they speak of ‘one who lives by the teaching’.
translation_text.an5.73:1.3: How is a mendicant who lives by the teaching defined?”
translation_text.an5.73:2.1: “Mendicant, take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching—
translation_text.an5.73:2.2: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an5.73:2.3: They spend too much time studying that teaching. They neglect retreat, and are not committed to internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an5.73:2.4: That mendicant is called one who studies a lot, not one who lives by the teaching.
translation_text.an5.73:3.1: Furthermore, a mendicant teaches Dhamma in detail to others as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.73:3.2: They spend too much time advocating that teaching. They neglect retreat, and are not committed to internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an5.73:3.3: That mendicant is called one who advocates a lot, not one who lives by the teaching.
translation_text.an5.73:4.1: Furthermore, a mendicant rehearses the teaching in detail as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.73:4.2: They spend too much time reciting that teaching. They neglect retreat, and are not committed to internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an5.73:4.3: That mendicant is called one who rehearses a lot, not one who lives by the teaching.
translation_text.an5.73:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant thinks about and considers the teaching in their heart, examining it with the mind as they learned and memorized it.
translation_text.an5.73:5.2: They spend too much time thinking about that teaching. They neglect retreat, and are not committed to internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an5.73:5.3: That mendicant is called one who thinks a lot, not one who lives by the teaching.
translation_text.an5.73:6.1: Take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching—
translation_text.an5.73:6.2: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an5.73:6.3: They don’t spend too much time studying that teaching. They don’t neglect retreat, and they’re committed to internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an5.73:6.4: That’s how a mendicant is one who lives by the teaching.
translation_text.an5.73:7.1: So, mendicant, I’ve taught you the one who studies a lot, the one who advocates a lot, the one who rehearses a lot, the one who thinks a lot, and the one who lives by the teaching.
translation_text.an5.73:7.2: Out of sympathy, I’ve done what a teacher should do who wants what’s best for their disciples.
translation_text.an5.73:7.3: Here are these roots of trees, and here are these empty huts. Practice absorption, mendicant! Don’t be negligent! Don’t regret it later! This is my instruction to you.”

variant_text.an5.73:7.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhu → yaṁ bhikkhu (sya-all, km, pts1ed)
